text:
Git branching is a powerful feature in version control that helps teams work on code without interfering with each other. When starting a new task, developers create a feature branch from the main codebase. This branch is like a separate workspace where they can make changes safely.

Once the feature is complete, the developer merges it back into the main branch. In many workflows, this is done through a pull request. A pull request allows team members to review the code, discuss changes, and catch errors before merging. This collaborative process ensures that the main code remains stable and high-quality. Using branches and pull requests creates a smooth, organized version control workflow for any project.
